structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints – ASHRAE 62.1 Compliance
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: VAV terminal units and SZVAV AHUs
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For ASHRAE 62.1 compliance, calculate zone minimum outdoor air and minimum airflow setpoints using the Ventilation Rate Procedure. Determine Ez based on DAT vs. space temperature. Vmin is set to Voz if AHU has been on 100% OA for 10 minutes and Vmin is AUTO, else 1.5*Voz if AUTO and not 100% OA, else scheduled Vmin. Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez, with modifications for unoccupied/unpopulated modes, occupancy sensor, and CO2 DCV. CO2 loop resets Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel for most terminal types, with specific VCO2-max for parallel fan-powered units. For SZVAV, MinOAsp = Voz and CO2 loop resets Vbz-P* only.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone requires mechanical ventilation and project complies with ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate Voz, Vmin, Vmin*, and Vbz-P* as per the detailed rules in Section 5.2.1.3.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, outdoor air and zone minimum setpoint s shall be calculated as follows: a. See Section 3.1.1.2 for zone ventilation setpoint s. b. Determine zone air distribution effectiveness Ez . 1. If the DAT at the terminal unit is less than or equal to zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzC (d efault to 1.0 if no value is scheduled). 2. If the DAT at the terminal unit is greater than zone space temperature, Ez shall be equal to EzH (default to 0.8 if no value is scheduled). c. Vbz-P* is the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor a irflow. The normal value of Vbz -P* shall be Vbz -P. Vbz-A* is the area component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow. The normal value of Vbz -A* shall be Vbz -A. d. Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes; 2. Else shall be equal to 1.5 * Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler is not supplying 100% outdoor air; 3. Else shall be equal Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2 . e. The occupied minimum airflow Vmin * shall be equal to Vmin except as noted in Section 5.2.1.3.f . f. The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz -A* + Vbz -P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz -A* and Vbz -P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority: 1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode , and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0. 2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occ upied -standby mode is permitted: Vbz -P* = 0, Vbz -A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0 . 3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied -standby mode is not permitted: Vbz -P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin. 4. If the zone has a CO 2 sensor : i. See Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 for CO 2 setpoint s. ii. During Occupied Mode , a P-only loop shall maintain CO 2 concentration at setpoint ; reset from 0% at setpoint minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint . iii. Loop is disabled and outp ut set to zero when the zone is not in Occupied Mode . iv. For cooling -only VAV terminal units, reheat VAV terminal units, constant -volume series fan -powered terminal units, dual -duct VAV terminal units with mixing control and inlet airflow sensors, dual -duct VAV terminal units with mixing control and a discharge airflow sensor, or dual -duct VAV terminal units with cold -duct minimum control: (a) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow (Vbz -P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool -max at 100% loop output. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output. v. For parallel fan -powered terminal units: (a) Determine VCO2 -max as follows: (1) When the Zone State is cooling, VCO2 -max is equal to the maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool -max. (2) When the Zone State is heating or deadband , VCO2 -max is equal to Vcool -max minus the parallel fan airflow (b) The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint VCO2 -max at 100% loop outp ut. Vbz -P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz -P at 100% loop output. vi. For SZVAV AHUs : (a) The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz -P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz -P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Sensor Fault Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 3 alarm if CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or if zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and CO2 exceeds 600 ppm (sensor may be out of calibration). Also generate a Level 3 alarm if CO2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: CO2 < 300 ppm, or Unoccupied >2 hours and CO2 >600 ppm, or CO2 > setpoint +10% for >10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 3 alarm with appropriate text.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: CO2 sensor out of calibration or ventilation issue.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For zones with CO 2 sensors: a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration. b. If the CO 2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minute s, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: For zones with CO 2 sensors: a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration. b. If the CO 2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minute s, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: For zones with CO 2 sensors: a. If the CO 2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO 2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration. b. If the CO 2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minute s, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Specifies fault diagnostic rules for CO2 sensor alarms with thresholds and conditions, directly from Section 5.2.2.3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) Pulse Width Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than the lowest controllable airflow Vm, pulse width modulate the airflow setpoint. TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Total cycle time TCT = 15 minutes (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open period, Vspt* = Vm; during closed period, Vspt* = 0 and VAV damper control loop disabled with output 0. At end of closed period, command damper to last open position before re-enabling loop. To avoid synchronized TAV, start with initial open period of RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number 0.0–1.0.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Implement TAV pulse width modulation as described.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation ( TAV ) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period . During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint Vspt * shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of the following : d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period. During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper con trol loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling the control loop. g. During TAV mode, e ach cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm. h. When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAV Mode Override of Active Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint Vspt shall be overridden to Vspt*.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt* .

evidence_quote: When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt* .
evidence_text: When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt* .
judge_reason: Explicitly states the override of active airflow setpoint during TAV mode, direct quote from Section 5.2.2.2.
